summary: The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held its first meeting of 2023, handling old business including approval of two resolutions for previously decided variances, adopting a new 2023 meeting schedule moving meetings to the fourth Monday of each month, transitioning to digital meeting minutes, and electing officers for the year. The board also noted openings for two alternate BZA members.
decisions: Approved December 19, 2022 video meeting minutes (with two abstentions)
Approved transition to digital meeting minutes going forward
Approved Resolution R063-2022 (variance for Victoria Rodney Siebel at 6790 Gaynor Road for lot width variance)
Approved Resolution R-002-2023 (area variance for Troy E. Smith at 6676 Wood Street for accessory structure placement)
Accepted the 2023 BZA meeting schedule (fourth Monday of each month)
Elected Ed Sigla as Chair for 2023
Elected Derek Holmes as Vice Chair for 2023
Elected Tom Currie as Secretary for 2023
action_items: Harry Engel to present BZA member term expirations to the Board of Trustees
Staff to seek two alternate BZA members to fill openings
Board members to email Harry with known scheduling conflicts in advance
Staff to continue zoning resolution revision workshops with Board of Trustees and Zoning Commission
notable_topics: Approval of resolutions for two previously decided variances (Siebel lot width and Smith accessory structure)
Transition from paper to digital meeting minutes
2023 BZA meeting schedule adoption (moved to fourth Monday)
Election of BZA Chair, Vice Chair, and Secretary for 2023
Openings for two alternate BZA members
Ongoing Goshen Township zoning resolution revision and workshop process
Discussion of BZA membership terms and quorum concerns
notes: The meeting was largely administrative with no new variance cases heard. Staff member Harry Engel indicated he would likely miss meetings through June due to personal schedule conflicts. One unnamed board member is reportedly on leave and may resign, which would move Gary Theodore from alternate to full member. The Board of Trustees has begun a zoning resolution revision process described as a 3-4 month undertaking involving workshop-level public meetings.
title: BZA 01.23.23
